---
title: "Hemlock Witchgrass (Dichanthelium patulum)"
slug: "dichanthelium-patulum"
group: "Plantae"
image: "https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/474819995/medium.jpg"        # keep for thumbnails
image-alt: "Hemlock Witchgrass"  # keep for a11y
tax_kingdom: "Plantae"
tax_class: "Liliopsida"
tax_order: "Poales"
tax_family: "Poaceae"
categories: ["Plantae", "Liliopsida", "Poales", "Poaceae"]   # these drive the chips + filtering
freeze: true
---

<figure class="species-figure">

<img src="https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/474819995/medium.jpg" alt="Hemlock Witchgrass" class="species-photo"/>

<figcaption class="photo-credit">

Photo: <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/people/hoskissbirder">
hoskissbirder</a> (CC BY-NC) via <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/observations/264384890">iNaturalist</a>

</figcaption>

</figure>

## Taxonomy

-   **Class:** Liliopsida
-   **Order:** Poales
-   **Family:** Poaceae

## Occurrence at DeLuca Preserve

-   [DeLuca Bio Blitz Species Observations](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?project_id=191727&subview=map&taxon_id=1210969)

## Conservation & rarity context

-   IUCN: Not Listed
-   **Rarity note:** Common species in Florida found in woodlands, flatwoods, and shaded disturbed areas.

## Natural history notes

A perennial grass characterized by its slender stems, broad leaves, and open, loosely branching seed heads. In Florida, it is found in a variety of habitats including woodlands, flatwoods, and shaded disturbed areas. It grows during the warmer months and contributes to groundcover in native plant communities.

## References / links

-   [iNaturalist species page](https://www.inaturalist.org/taxa/1210969-Dichanthelium-patulum)
-   [Local profile](https://florida.plantatlas.usf.edu/plant/species/3080)
